title: Griggor Tower
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Griggor_Tower.jpg]] |name=Griggor Tower |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Gamandar]][[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=One blue, one red implant |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Tower is an arrogant fool, and a brute…|Darth Vader|[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]]}} '''Griggor Tower''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] who led the subjugation of the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Under Tower's command, Imperial forces rendered [[Telfrey]] [[Decimation of Telfrey|uninhabitable]] and enslaved the people of [[Gamandar]], subsequently making the [[Planet/Legends|world]] his headquarters. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Tower also devastated nearby [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] by generating a massive [[Iskalon Effect|tidal wave]], after learning that the [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]] was harboring a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents that included [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Outraged that Tower had endangered Skywalker's life, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]—who wanted the Rebel alive for his own purposes—ordered the Admiral's execution. ==Biography== {{Quote|Lord Vader... back in the detention area... there are some Rebel prisoners. P-perhaps one of them could be..."
"The man I seek is not among them. Whoever they are, they shall keep until my eturn. Guards, execute Admiral Tower. Transmission ends.|Griggor Tower and Darth Vader|}} A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Griggor Tower joined the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and reached the lofty rank of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] by the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Tower led an upswell of Imperial activity in the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]], which attracted the attention of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Tower met the growing unrest with brutal subjugation, occupying the waterworld of [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]], [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] the populace of neighboring [[Gamandar]]—which became the site of his [[Gamandar Citadel|citadel]]—and rendering [[Telfrey]], [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Rebel provocateurs [[Yom Argo]] and [[Tay Vanis]], uninhabitable. After the devastation of Telfrey, the aquatic [[Species/Legends|species]] of the [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]] sent ambassadors to Gamandar to meet with Tower—the Admiral smashed their water tanks and left them to suffocate, proclaiming that was the only peace their attempts at diplomacy would yield. However, the Iskalonians continued to give Tower pause. Reading further into a comment made by one of the Iskalonian ambassadors, Tower began to research the "[[Iskalon Effect]]," the concept that a world-spanning tidal wave on Iskalon could be triggered by a single large impact. [[File:Tower1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Griggor Tower in his citadel on Gamandar.]] In time, Tower would find reason to cause the Iskalon Effect. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents arrived on Iskalon in order to investigate Vanis' whereabouts, and several of their number—led by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]—followed a tip from an Iskalonian named [[Kendle]] to search further on Gamandar. Unbeknownst to them, Kendle was an informant for Tower, who notified the Admiral that the Rebels were en route. Once Tower received word that the Rebel ship was more than halfway between Iskalon and Gamandar, he ordered a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] launch that triggered the Iskalon Effect and devastated Iskalonian society, including the destruction of the planet's the main settlement of [[Pavillion]] and the death of the School's ruling [[Primor (Inleshat)|Primor]]. Not long after, Tower entertained Calrissian—posing as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named "[[Barpotomous Drebble/Legends|Drebble]]"—at his citadel, as the Rebels probed for information on Vanis. Knowing their true identities, Tower [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] them almost immediately.[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] However, Tower had made a crucial miscalculation. One of the Rebels left behind on Iskalon during the tidal wave was [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the hero of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], who was a highly desired target of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Vader was furious to learn that Tower had endangered Skywalker's life, and made haste immediately to exact punishment. Tower had begun interrogating the captured Rebels for information on Vanis when he was interrupted by [[K-3PX]], a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] assigned to the citadel that was secretly in Vader's service—K-3PX, with a squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stomtroopers]] in tow, informed Tower that he was under arrest on Vader's orders. A disheveled Tower was dragged in front of Vader via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]], and despite the discovery that survivors had been discovered in the ruins of Pavillion, Vader had Tower summarily [[Death/Legends|execution]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I've been looking forward to this day for a long time... ever since that imbecilic ambassador explained his people's philosophy, and warned me about the Iskalon Effect... I knew that the day would come when they'd give me a reason to set it off...|Griggor Tower|}} A one-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]—his missing eye was replaced with a distinctive [[Color/Legends|red]] prosthetic—Griggor Tower was ruthless and violent in his subjugation and oppression of the Iskalon system. Tower had utter contempt for the Iskalonian School and its people, waiting for a reason to annihilate them with the Iskalon Effect. While these qualities served him well in ruling Iskalon with an iron fist, they did not always endear him to his superiors—Darth Vader in particular regarded him as a arrogant brute. Despite his bluster, Tower retained a subservient attitude towards Lord Vader, although his desperate attempts at appeasement did not save him from execution on Gamandar. ==Behind the scenes== Griggor Tower appeared as a primary antagonist in three issues of the original [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] comic series in [[1983]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and illustrated by [[Ron Frenz]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 74}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 75|''Star Wars'' (1977) 75]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 75}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 76|''Star Wars'' (1977) 76]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 76}} ==Sources== *{{GalaxyCite|11|ComicScan|ComicScan: Paint Him Black}} {{Imo}} *{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Griggor Tower}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Griggor Tower }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tower, Griggor}} [[Category:Cyborgs]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy admirals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
